primary_task.configuration: Codex 5.6 / GPT-5.6, Sol Ultra mode, as explicitly selected for the primary build task

usage.codex: Primary engineering environment for repository analysis, architecture, implementation, tests, verification, and submission materials.
usage.gpt_5_6: The reasoning model used materially inside the primary Codex build task for architecture decisions, deterministic contracts, adversarial cases, fixture design, and product narrative.

material_contributions.description: Compared the supplied TEMPO v1.3 archive, pinned VEMO commit, and earlier commercial-agent contract; separated implemented mechanisms from specifications and excluded private course-derived material.
Detected the circular dependency between readiness and authorization, then designed the two-stage eligibility/warrant boundary and hash-drift semantics.
Reframed the product from a developer-only guardrail into a cross-functional Work & Productivity workflow while preserving the deterministic technical proof.
Built the schema, evidence, ledger, guard, receipt, CLI, and verdict surfaces and translated failure modes into adversarial automated cases with stable exit semantics.
Designed a credential-free fixture that demonstrates insufficient evidence, authorization separation, one bounded start, terminal drift, a verification receipt, and a blank human verdict section.
Mapped the official judging criteria and public OpenAI UX guidance to an honest submission narrative, preserving explicit blockers for repository publication, video upload, and Devpost submission.

evidence_boundary.note: Run /feedback in the primary task and record its returned value before submission. The recorded openai/gpt-5.6 commercial proposal is a labeled fixture and is not evidence of a live runtime call.
